food_subgroup: Fast food burger — beef patty with cheese, mushroom, and condiments on a bun

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger class (FOODON_00002774). No specific class exists for a double cheeseburger with mushroom topping; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers hamburger as a generic composite food. No specific code for double cheeseburger with mushroom; closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for a double cheeseburger with mushroom and creamy sauce; null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger as served at McDonald's Singapore, approximately 200 g, comprising two beef patties, two slices of cheese, grilled mushrooms, black pepper mayo, and a sesame-seed bun.

nutrient_source.title: Fast foods, cheeseburger; double, regular patty, with condiments and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry 'Fast foods, cheeseburger; double, regular patty, with condiments and vegetables' (FDC ID 175188) used as the closest approved analogue for a double cheeseburger with mushroom and creamy sauce. Mushroom and black pepper mayo toppings are not present in the USDA entry; the base macronutrient profile is considered representative. Values are adapted from per-100 g USDA data. Confidence is medium due to the topping difference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger. GI of 66 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a standard hamburger / cheeseburger on a white-flour bun (GI ~66, medium category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 45.0) / 100 ≈ 29.7, using estimated 45 g available carbohydrate per 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~490 mg per 100 g; ~980 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item, as a single serving provides approximately 980 mg sodium — roughly 43% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and the black pepper mayo sauce adds further sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load (~29.7 per serving) combined with refined carbohydrate bun and added sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this burger as an occasional food only, as the refined-flour bun and added condiments produce a glycemic load of approximately 29.7 per serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side and limiting portion frequency is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.8 g per 100 g; ~11.6 g per serving), trans fat (~0.5 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this burger, as each serving contributes approximately 11.6 g saturated fat (58% of the AHA daily limit), ~1.0 g trans fat, and ~980 mg sodium, all of which are independently associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger is a limited-time fast-food burger served at McDonald's Singapore, featuring two grilled beef patties stacked on a toasted sesame-seed bun, each topped with a slice of processed cheddar cheese. The burger is distinguished by a generous portion of juicy grilled mushrooms and a swirl of creamy black pepper mayonnaise sauce, which together impart an earthy, savoury, and mildly spiced flavour profile. The texture contrasts soft, yielding bun with the firm, slightly charred patties and the tender mushroom pieces. Nutritionally, the burger is energy-dense at approximately 263 kcal per 100 g (around 526 kcal per standard 200 g serving), with meaningful protein (~13.9 g/100 g) supporting satiety, but also substantial saturated fat (~5.8 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g), and sodium (~490 mg/100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The refined-flour bun contributes to a medium glycemic index of approximately 66 and a glycemic load of ~29.7 per serving. The burger is certified Halal and is sold at McDonald's outlets across Singapore, including Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay locations, typically priced between SGD 7.30 and SGD 9.35 depending on meal configuration. It is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally within a balanced dietary pattern.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger?
Is the McDonald's Mushroom Double Cheeseburger Halal?
What is the sodium content of the Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of a McDonald's double cheeseburger?
How much protein is in the Mushroom Double Cheeseburger?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger available in Singapore?
What sauce is on the Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for the Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy entry for a double cheeseburger with condiments and vegetables (FDC ID 175188), which is the closest defensible analogue. The mushroom and black pepper mayo toppings are not captured in the USDA entry, introducing a moderate degree of uncertainty. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogue for a standard cheeseburger on a white-flour bun. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175188 (fast foods, double cheeseburger with condiments and vegetables). The grilled mushroom and black pepper mayo toppings are not reflected in the USDA source; actual values for the McDonald's Singapore product may differ. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used. 5. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a standard hamburger/cheeseburger on a white-flour bun; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 6. The input food_group was listed as 'Vegetables', which is incorrect for a beef cheeseburger; corrected to 'Mixed' per Veritas food group taxonomy. 7. The burger is tagged Halal per field survey cuisine tags and McDonald's Singapore Halal certification; is_halal set to true. 8. Serving size of 200 g is an estimate based on typical McDonald's double cheeseburger weight; no official weight was available from an approved source.

improvement_actions_taken: Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served dishes.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check.
Sourced nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175188 as closest approved analogue; documented adaptation.
Estimated GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogue for standard cheeseburger; documented estimation basis.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Added three health context entries with specific clinical guidance for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease.
Wrote LLM-ready natural language description exceeding 200 characters.
Verified no zero-macro values requiring justification phrases (all core macros are non-zero).
Set is_halal to true based on field survey Halal tag and McDonald's Singapore Halal certification.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent' to comply with allowed values.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
